description: Civics and Citizenship concept progression map — Knowledge and Understanding, Year 7 to 10. Three ACARA sub-strands: Government and democracy, Laws and citizens, Citizenship diversity and identity.
subject: Civics and Citizenship

standards.title: Government foundations
standards.description: the key features of Australia's system of government, including democracy, the Australian Constitution, responsible government and federalism
standards.y_goal: Students explain the key features of Australia's system of government including democracy, the Constitution, responsible government and federalism.

standards.title: Democratic freedoms
standards.description: the characteristics of Australia's democracy, including freedom of speech, association, assembly, religion and movement
standards.y_goal: Students explain the characteristics of Australia's democracy including freedom of speech, association, assembly, religion and movement.

standards.title: Legal system foundations
standards.description: the key principles and features of the Australian legal system, including the Australian Constitution, the rule of law and the court system
standards.y_goal: Students explain the key principles and features of the Australian legal system including the Constitution, rule of law and court system.

standards.title: Pluralism and cultural origins
standards.description: how Australia's secular democracy and pluralist, multi-faith society draws upon diverse cultural origins, including Christian and Western heritage, distinct First Nations Australian histories and cultures, and migrant communities
standards.y_goal: Students explain how Australia's secular democracy and pluralist society draws upon diverse cultural origins including First Nations histories and migrant communities.

standards.title: Democratic values
standards.description: how values based on freedom, respect, fairness and equality of opportunity can support social cohesion and democracy within Australian society
standards.y_goal: Students explain how values of freedom, respect, fairness and equality support social cohesion and democracy.

standards.title: Democratic participation
standards.description: how Australians are informed about and participate in democracy
standards.y_goal: Students explain how Australians are informed about and participate in democracy.

standards.title: Political representation
standards.description: the role of political parties and independent representatives in Australian democracy, including elections and the formation of governments
standards.y_goal: Students explain the role of political parties and independent representatives in Australian democracy including elections and government formation.

standards.title: Law-making processes
standards.description: the characteristics of laws and how laws are made in Australia through parliaments (statutory law) and through the courts (common law)
standards.y_goal: Students explain the characteristics of laws and how laws are made through parliaments and courts.

standards.title: Types of law
standards.description: the types of law in Australia, including criminal law and civil law, and the place of First Nations Australian customary law
standards.y_goal: Students explain the types of law in Australia including criminal, civil and First Nations customary law.

standards.title: Culture, religion and citizenship
standards.description: how culture and religion may influence individuals' and groups' perceptions and expressions of citizenship and their actions as citizens
standards.y_goal: Students explain how culture and religion influence perceptions and expressions of citizenship.

standards.title: National identity debates
standards.description: different experiences of, perspectives on and debates about Australia's national identity and citizenship, including the perspectives of First Nations Australians as owners of their respective nations, and of different migrant groups
standards.y_goal: Students explain different perspectives on and debates about Australia's national identity including First Nations and migrant perspectives.

standards.title: Constitution and democratic processes
standards.description: the role of the Australian Constitution in providing the basis for Australia's federal system of government and democratic processes, including institutions, and the process for constitutional change through a referendum
standards.y_goal: Students explain the role of the Australian Constitution in Australia's federal system and the process for constitutional change through referendum.

standards.title: Legislative policy processes
standards.description: the legislative processes through which federal government policy is shaped, developed and implemented
standards.y_goal: Students explain the legislative processes through which federal government policy is shaped, developed and implemented.

standards.title: Court jurisdictions and tribunals
standards.description: the key features and jurisdictions of Australia's court system, and the operations of courts and tribunals
standards.y_goal: Students explain the key features and jurisdictions of Australia's court system and the operations of courts and tribunals.

standards.title: Trials, roles and rights
standards.description: the role of courts, judges, lawyers and juries in trials, and the rights of the accused and the rights of victims
standards.y_goal: Students explain the role of courts, judges, lawyers and juries in trials and the rights of the accused and victims.

standards.title: Civic and global participation
standards.description: how and why individuals and groups, including community, religious and cultural groups, participate in and contribute to civic life in Australia and to global citizenship
standards.y_goal: Students explain how and why individuals and groups participate in civic life in Australia and contribute to global citizenship.

standards.title: Media influence on identity
standards.description: the influence of a range of media, including social media, in shaping identity and attitudes to diversity
standards.y_goal: Students explain the influence of media including social media in shaping identity and attitudes to diversity.

standards.title: Comparing systems of government
standards.description: the key features and values of Australia's system of government compared with at least one other democratic or non-democratic system of government in the Asia-Pacific region
standards.y_goal: Students compare the key features and values of Australia's system of government with at least one other system in the Asia-Pacific region.

standards.title: Regional and global government roles
standards.description: the Australian Government's role and responsibilities at a regional and global level
standards.y_goal: Students explain the Australian Government's role and responsibilities at a regional and global level.

standards.title: Parliament, High Court and rights
standards.description: the role of the parliament and the High Court of Australia in protecting rights under the Constitution, common law, and through federal and state statute law
standards.y_goal: Students explain the role of parliament and the High Court in protecting rights under the Constitution, common law and statute law.

standards.title: International law and policy
standards.description: how Australia's international legal obligations shape Australian law and government policies, including those relating to First Nations Australians, and the issues related to the application of these obligations
standards.y_goal: Students explain how Australia's international legal obligations shape Australian law and government policies.

standards.title: Sustaining democracy and cohesion
standards.description: the challenges to and ways of sustaining a resilient democracy and a cohesive society in Australia and/or in our region or globally
standards.y_goal: Students explain the challenges to and ways of sustaining a resilient democracy and cohesive society in Australia and globally.

progression_threads.description: Concept dependency chains following ACARA sub-strand headings.
